project_information.project_name: Roaring Fork Community Irrigation Efficiency and River Health Project
project_information.project_location_address_and_county: Site 1: 892 County Road 115, Glenwood Springs, CO 81601, Garfield County; Site 2: 4273 Highway 133, Carbondale, CO 81623, Pitkin County
project_information.brief_project_summary_limit_150_words: This project will modernize two aging agricultural irrigation systems in the Roaring Fork watershed to improve water efficiency, reduce diversions during low-flow periods, and enhance late-season river health. The Roaring Fork Watershed Alliance is partnering with three family-owned farms and a small ditch company to replace leaky open ditches with buried, pressurized pipe; install telemetry-controlled headgates; and implement soil moisture monitoring to fine-tune irrigation scheduling. These upgrades are expected to reduce diversion needs by 25–35%, returning an estimated 150–200 acre-feet of water annually to the Roaring Fork River and Cattle Creek during critical late summer months. The project includes a strong community education component—field days, bilingual workshops (English/Spanish), and student monitoring projects with local schools—to demonstrate practical, replicable approaches for balancing working agriculture with river conservation in a semi-arid, snowmelt-dependent basin.
